summary:
The auriculotemporal nerve primarily supplies sensation to the posterior and inferior aspects of the TMJ capsule. This information highlights its importance clinically due to involvement in sensations related to jaw movements and surgical considerations nearby.
The answer provided by Agent2 can be summarized as follows:

- The auriculotemporal nerve mainly supplies sensation to specific areas of the TMJ capsule.
- Clinically significant because it affects jaw movement sensations and surgery planning. To keep within your instructions strictly limiting summary to two sentences while focusing only on critical details pertinent to answering "Sensory nerve supply of capsule of TMJ," here’s a concise version based solely on those requirements:

- The auriculotemporal nerve predominantly innervates portions of the TMJ capsule.
- Its role is important for detecting sensations associated with jaw function. 

This maintains focus directly on core medical relevance without extraneous detail or additional conclusions beyond what was asked regarding sensory nerves supplying the temporal mandibular joint capsules.
